text.title: Persona Building Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Persona Building methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Ship exactly 3 personas: primary, secondary, negative; not more, not fewer.
rule.rationale: Two personas miss the disqualifier (negative); >3 fragments GTM messaging.

rule.statement: Every persona MUST cite >=5 verbatim interview quotes; quotes from a single interview do not count as 5.
rule.rationale: Single-source personas are fiction; cross-interview citations are the minimum evidence floor.

rule.statement: Personas are written around JTBD (job-to-be-done) statements, not age/income demographics alone.
rule.rationale: Demographics weakly predict purchase behaviour; jobs predict it strongly.

rule.statement: Every persona MUST include a kill-criteria block naming traits that disqualify a user from being this persona.
rule.rationale: Without kill criteria, the persona slowly absorbs every user type.

rule.statement: Top-3 pains MUST be ranked, not equal-weighted; pain #1 drives onboarding copy.
rule.rationale: Equal-weighted pains produce diluted copy that addresses none of them well.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
